extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim won stage 3 sprint after EWAN Caleb crashed himself out touching MERLIER's wheel in final 200m, taking SAGAN down with him.
tactical_patterns: VAN DER POEL Matthew rode yellow jersey leadout successfully at 1300+ watts, proving GC leaders can execute sprint trains.
THOMAS Geraint remounted after early crash despite initially lying down, suggesting team orders override rider instinct for Tour GC contenders.
Final 5km position battle intensity exceeded normal sprint chaos, causing multiple sprinters including CAVENDISH to miss selection entirely.
Technical descent inside final 10km fractured peloton before sprint, making positioning before descent more critical than positioning at 3km.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: overeager sprint initiation touching MERLIER's wheel suggests desperation from poor positioning despite running bigger 54-tooth chainring for speed
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: executed full leadout in yellow jersey after two hard stages, revealing exceptional recovery capacity and team commitment priority
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: chose massive 55-tooth chainring but never reached final selection, indicating positioning weakness more limiting than top-end speed
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: positioned well until EWAN collision, suggesting form returning but sprint chaos remains existential risk
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: lost time despite sprint finish due to final 5km positioning chaos, revealing potential weakness in high-speed technical approaches
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: early crash requiring initial prone position but continued suggests compromised GC campaign from day 3
